# Cellular-Automata

> A MATLAB cellular automaton that simulates threshold-based cell toppling and neighbor redistribution on a two-dimensional grid, with animated visualization of the grid's evolution.

Cellular-Automata is a small MATLAB project (originally written in the MATLAB R2015 era). It runs a square grid of integer-valued cells: each cell accumulates a value, and when the value crosses a fixed threshold the cell topples, shedding part of its content and redistributing the excess to neighboring cells with a downward directional bias. Overflow that leaves the grid edges is tracked as boundary accumulation. This toppling-and-redistribution rule is the mechanism behind sandpile / chip-firing style cellular automata. The grid is redrawn at each step and played back as a MATLAB movie animation.

## Key concepts

- Grid-based cellular automaton on a 10x10 (configurable) matrix of integer cells.
- Threshold-triggered toppling: cells exceeding a threshold shed and redistribute excess to neighbors.
- Directional (downward) redistribution with boundary overflow tracking.
- State matrix plus actuation matrix drive each update step.
- Iterative evolution until no cell exceeds the threshold, with 3-D history arrays of every step.
- Animated visualization of the grid via MATLAB movie frames.

## Files

- README.md: Project overview, file roles, requirements, and how to run in MATLAB.
- wmsk.m: Main actuation loop — toppling rule, neighbor redistribution, boundary overflow, and frame capture.
- Rul.m: Per-cell state construction and toppling rule.
- StActu.m: Iterates the update until the grid stabilizes, stacking each step into 3-D history arrays.
- Shift.m: Row-shift helper used during redistribution.
- mskgrp.m: Renders the grid as a labeled color plot for each animation frame.
- TEST.m: Test driver that sets up an initial grid and starts the animation.

## Run

- Requires MATLAB.
- Run `TEST` in MATLAB to set up a sample grid and start the animation.
- Call `StActu(stateMatrix, actuationMatrix)` to iterate on custom matrices, or `wmsk(initialMatrix, actuationMatrix, dim, movrep, movra)` for the animated actuation.
